description: lib::run() is still the §8 skeleton (it resolves a verb and prints Op::plan; lib.rs says 'wiring it to the engine is a later phase'). No open phase owns connecting argv → a real op, so NO verb is invokable end-to-end yet and the epic cannot self-host / migrate. Scope: parse argv into (verb, positional id(s), flags) — the §10 front-door flags --parent/--gates/--needs (which write the {id,on} edges via change::Create's already-built reciprocal authoring), -m/-d/--over, --as identity, priority/tags; resolve the store + operating checkout + change worktree (terminus); build the right change::BaseChange per verb; resolve the registry's committed NN- plugin sets for each op-phase; run lifecycle::Engine::seal (mutating) / ::diffless (reads) with §14 rollback; render §9 human/--json output. This is also what first exercises the gating plugin (bl-70f5) against a LIVE claim/close op — today it is only tested against hand-built §7 payloads. Front-door flags, predicates (ready/closeable), taskfile IO, the engine, change base-changes, registry, wire, and the gate all already exist; this is the integration seam that calls them. Plugins are subprocess hook binaries and do NOT extend bl's parser (§10), so all flag parsing is core.
title: Wire bl dispatch: run() → §8 engine + CLI arg/flag parsing
